rulings.Case Title: KENNETH THORNTON VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Unigard Insurance Company And Fireman'S Fund Insurance Company'S Motion For Leave Of Court To File Complaint In Intervention To Name Fireman'S Fund Insurance Company And Unigard Insurance Company As Intervenors
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, July 16, 2009, line 1, Defendants Unigard Insurance Co. and Fireman's Fund Insur. Co. Motion for leave of Court to file complaint in intervention to name Fireman's Fund Insurance Co. and Unigard Insur. Co. as Intervenors. (Per notice of motion filed on 6/10/09; calendared for 7/6/09 in error). The Court's July 6, 2009 ruling taking this motion Off Calendar is vacated. The Court Grants the instant motion. No opposition filed and movant has a right to intervene per Reliance Ins. Co. vs. Superior Court (2000) 84 Cal.App. 4th 383. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Awaed Of Attorney Fees Incurred In Collection Of Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, July 16, 2009, line 6, PLAINTIFF ROBERT SPRAGUE Motion For An Award Of Attorney Fees Incurred In Collection Of Judgment. Motion is granted. Plaintiff awarded a total of $11,367 as reasonable fees and costs. All future collection motions to be brought in the discovery department per San Francisco Local Rule 10(A)(3). =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: ATEL VENTURES, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION VS. REVVER, INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, July 16, 2009, line 10, DEFENDANT REVVER, INC., and, LIVEUNIVERSE, INC. MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND. Motion is denied without prejudice. Defendant's motion lacks competent evidence because the supporting declarations do not comply with the subscription requirement of CCP section 2015.5. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: ROSENFELD LAW CORPORATION et al VS. UZI ALOUSH
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Uzi Aloush, Ipil Ventures, Inc., And Ileverage Group, Llc To Disqualify Pltfs' Counsel; Request For Judicial Notice
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, July 16, 2009, line 15, DEFENDANT Uzi Aloush, Ipil Ventures, Inc., and Ileverage Group, LLC Motion To Disqualify Plaintiffs' Counsel. Motion is denied. Insufficient evidence of any attorney-client relationship. Furthermore, defendant fails to show that the antecedent relationship was substantially related to the instant action. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, July 16, 2009, line 16, DEFENDANT DEBORAH FARRINGTON, JEANNE SULLIVAN, STARVEST PARTNERS II, L.P., STARVEST PARTNERS II (PARALLEL), L.P., STARVEST PARTNERS II (ADVISORS), L.P. MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE. Motion to admit counsel pro hac vice is granted. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: EMPORTAL, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION VS. STARVEST PARTNERS II, L.P.,A DELAWARE LIMITED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Stay The Complaint The Defts On The Ground Of Inconveniet Forum
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, July 16, 2009, line 16, DEFENDANT DEBORAH FARRINGTON, JEANNE SULLIVAN, STARVEST PARTNERS II, L.P., STARVEST PARTNERS II (PARALLEL), L.P., STARVEST PARTNERS II (ADVISORS), L.P. Motion To Stay The Complaint On The Ground Of Inconveniet Forum. Motion to stay is granted. New York is a suitable alternative forum. All California defendants stipulate to New York jurisdiction. New York was the first filed suit and discovery is progressing. Plaintiff filed an answer in the New York action, consenting to jurisdiction. Plaintiff may file a cross-complaint for damages in the New York action. =(302/CWW)
